title: Morris Bush
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Citation}} {{Person |image=[[File:Dengar2.jpg]] |name=Morris Bush |birth=[[June 3]], [[1930s|1930]]{{BookCite|container=Morrie's career on screen with the greats|title=Isle of Thanet Gazette|date=1999-09-10|pages=p. 17|link=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0004721/19990910/017/0017}} [[Wikipedia:Pimlico|Pimlico]] [[Wikipedia:England|England]] |death=[[August 22]], [[1999]]
[[Wikipedia:Thanet District|Thanet]], England |listedpronouns= |nationality=British |occupation=*[[Wikipedia:Boxing|Boxer]] *[[Wikipedia:Actor|Actor]] |companies= |website= }} '''Morris Bush''' or Maurice 'Morrie' Bush ([[June 3]], [[1930]] - [[August 22]], [[1999]]) was an [[Wikipedia:Actor|actor]] who played [[Dengar]]. ==Biography== British boxer-turned-actor Maurice Bush (who was occasionally billed as Morris Bush) was a supporting player in a number of British films and television series from the 1950s to the 1990s. Bush was selected to play mummy-like [[bounty hunter]] [[Dengar]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] ([[1980]]), uncredited and without any speaking lines. Dengar was intended to appear again in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] ([[1983]]), as one of the background characters on [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[sail barge]], and his footage was filmed. However, save for a small appearance shortly after [[Luke Skywalker]] falls into the [[Rancor]] pit, Dengar was edited from the final movie. Dengar's face in ''Return of the Jedi'' is never clearly seen, and it was assumed by many that Bush did not play the role on this occasion until [[Jeremy Bulloch]] confirmed that it was Bush at [[Celebration Europe II]]'s panel, "The Fett-Tastic Four." His appearances would be used as [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] background for the character that would take place shortly after the events at [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]]. (It was Dengar who saved [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] from the [[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc]]). It is also highly likely that Morris Bush was on the set of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] ([[1977]]), as [[David Prowse|Dave Prowse]] recounts that Morris Bush subbed for him as Darth Vader for the sequence immediately following the death of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. (Bush's feet are shown kicking Kenobi's empty cloak). ==Works== ===Filmography=== {| {{Prettytable}} ! Year !! Title !! Contribution(s) !! Notes |- |[[1977]]||[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]||[[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]]||Stood in for [[David Prowse]] immediately after [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] died. |- |[[1980]]||[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]||[[Dengar]]||Uncredited |- |[[1983]]||[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]||Dengar|| |} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|49|Trail of the Bounty Hunters}} {{C|as "Moray Bush"}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{IMDb|name/nm0124176}} {{Interlang |de=Morris Bush |pl=Morris Bush |tr=Morris Bush }} [[Category:Deceased actors]] [[Category:Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back actors]] [[Category:Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi actors]]
